estimated.note: Higher estimate includes unreported deaths in restricted areas
Slight variance due to delayed military casualty reports

parties.name: Iranian Civilians

estimated.note: Wide range: HRANA says 7,015; Iranian doctors network says 16,500-18,000; Iran International estimates up to 36,500. Trump cited 32,000 in State of the Union.

parties.arrestedNote: Mass arrests during Dec 2025–Jan 2026 protests. HRANA documented 41,800+ detained across 31 provinces.

conflicts.name: 2026 Iran War (Feb 28 - Present)
conflicts.period: February 28, 2026 - Ongoing (Day 40, 11:33 PM PDT — CEASEFIRE IN EFFECT)

parties.name: Iran

confirmed.source: Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera tracker / Iranian Red Crescent — Day 39 EVENING (April 7). 2,340+ killed since Feb. 28. +2 killed at Kashan railway bridge strike. IRGC Intel Chief Khademi + Quds Force cmdr Bagheri + missile cmdr Makram Atimi killed. Khorramshahr shipbuilding, Natanz IRGC HQ struck.
confirmed.confirmedMilitaryNote: Iran does not publish military casualty figures. The 0 reflects concealment, not absence of military deaths.

estimated.source: HRANA — Day 39 EVENING (April 7). 3,770 killed: ~1,752 civilians (including 256+ children), ~2,018 military/unclassified. Khorramshahr, Natanz, Shiraz targeted. 316+ healthcare centers damaged.
estimated.note: HRANA documents via field reports. Day 39 afternoon: 3,770 total. +2 Kashan railway bridge. 65 schools, 77+ healthcare facilities damaged (316+ per Health Ministry). Internet at ~4%. ~43+ officials killed including Atimi.

rumored.note: Hengaw (April 1): 7,300+ killed in first 34 days including 890 civilians. Previous 7th report (March 25): 6,530 killed. IDF Brig Gen Defrin claims 6,000+ IRGC killed and 15,000+ wounded. Upper range approaching 9,500. Internet blackout prevents verification. CEASEFIRE now in effect — casualties should stabilize.

socialSources.claim: 7th report (March 25): 6,530 killed — 5,890 military, 640 civilians. 190+ cities across 27 provinces targeted. Iranian security institutions systematically conceal casualty figures.
5th report (March 17): 5,300 killed — 4,789 military, 511 civilians. 120 minors, 160 women among dead. 178 cities across 25 provinces affected.
Day 23: 1,407 civilians killed including 214 children. More than 15% of total fatalities are children. Military casualties significantly higher than civilian count.
Over 1,400 civilians killed in first three weeks per HRANA documentation. True toll likely far higher due to internet blackout and government concealment.
IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed, 15,000 wounded, 70% of Iran's missile launchers destroyed. Plans through Passover (3+ more weeks).
Experts say actual death toll 'could be far greater as more details are released' — Iran's internet blackout prevents verification.
Iran's true casualty figures unknown as internet blackout hampers monitors. Connection worse than ever before — extremely difficult to get accurate data.

parties.injuredNote: 26,872+ injured per Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera tracker (Day 39 afternoon). +3 injured at Kashan railway bridge strike. Previous: +24 Alborz residential, +170 South Pars. 316+ healthcare centers damaged.
parties.missingNote: 61 sailors still missing from IRIS Dena — 87 bodies recovered and 32 rescued from ~180 crew after US submarine USS Charlotte torpedoed the frigate off Sri Lanka coast (March 4).
parties.displacedNote: 3.2M displaced across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz and border regions per UNHCR. Shelter capacity exceeded.
parties.arrestedNote: 20 arrested in NW Iran for alleged cooperation with Israel. 18 arrested for ties to Iran International (March 16-17).

parties.name: Israel

confirmed.source: Israeli government + IDF (Day 40 PM). 17 IDF soldiers killed. 21 civilians killed. 6,689+ wounded. +4 lightly injured Tel Sheva from post-ceasefire Iran missile barrage. +6 injured Nahariya from Hezbollah rockets (24 attack waves, 40+ rockets). Home Front Command wartime restrictions extended. CEASEFIRE IN EFFECT but Lebanon excluded.

estimated.source: INSS / Statista + Alma Center + Israeli emergency services (Day 39 PM)
estimated.note: Updated Day 38 PM: 17 IDF soldiers killed (confirmed). 23 civilians estimated (21 confirmed + possible unreported). +4 Haifa building (elderly couple 80s, man 40s, woman 35), +2 Ramat Gan cluster munitions (elderly couple 70s). Previous: 4 Nahal Brigade Lebanon, 1 Sgt Ben Zion, etc. Iran claims significantly higher but unverified.

rumored.note: Iranian state media claims significantly higher Israeli casualties — IRGC claims 40+ killed across 9 missile salvos. OSINT analysts report direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media. Israel's military censor restricts casualty reporting under wartime regulations.

socialSources.claim: Multiple videos show direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media — possible casualties suppressed by military censorship.
IRGC claims 40+ Israelis killed across 9 missile salvos — IDF only confirming 17.
2 killed in Ramat Gan as Iranian cluster missile hits multiple impact sites. Shrapnel rained on several neighborhoods.
Iranian missile strikes injure 180+ in Israel (Arad/Dimona), puncturing air defenses and shocking the public.

parties.injuredNote: 6,689+ injured since Feb. 28 per Alma Center / Israel Health Ministry (Day 40 PM). +4 lightly injured Tel Sheva from post-ceasefire Iranian missile barrage. +6 injured Nahariya from Hezbollah 24-wave rocket attack. Previous: +6 cluster munition salvos (Petah Tikva, Ramat Gan). 400+ Iranian attack waves total. 92% interception rate. CEASEFIRE IN EFFECT.
parties.displacedNote: ~5,000 Israelis displaced from homes due to missile strikes per Israeli media reports.

parties.name: United States

confirmed.source: Pentagon / NPR / Time (Day 37). 15 US service members killed since Feb 28: 13 combat deaths + 2 noncombat deaths. 365+ wounded. F-15E WSO RESCUED via commando raid in Isfahan (April 5). CIA ran deception campaign; US destroyed 2 stranded C-130s to prevent capture. A-10 pilot recovered earlier.

estimated.source: Pentagon / CENTCOM — 15 total: 13 combat + 2 non-combat deaths. F-15E WSO rescued April 5 via commando raid deep inside Iran.
estimated.note: 15 total US deaths during Operation Epic Fury: (1) Kuwait drone strike, March 1 — 6 killed: Capt. Cody Khork (35), SFC Noah Tietjens (42), SFC Nicole Amor (39), Spc. Declan Coady (20), Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien (45), CW3 Robert Marzan (54), all 103rd Sustainment Command. (2) Sgt. Benjamin Pennington (26) — wounded March 1 at Prince Sultan AB, Saudi Arabia, died March 8. (3) LCpl Kevin Melendez (~20, USMC) — died in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, non-hostile, under investigation. (4) Maj. Sorffly Davius (46, NY National Guard/NYPD officer) — died March 6 at Camp Buehring, Kuwait, health-related incident. (5) KC-135 crash, March 12 — 6 killed: Maj. John Klinner (33), Capt. Ariana Savino (31), Capt. Seth Koval (38), Capt. Curtis Angst (30), TSgt. Ashley Pruitt (34), TSgt. Tyler Simmons (28). Crash not due to hostile fire per CENTCOM. ~200 wounded across 7 countries (March 17), 10 seriously. 180+ returned to duty.

rumored.note: IRGC claims 650+ US killed/wounded in first 2 days (largely debunked by Jerusalem Post). Ali Larijani claimed US soldiers were captured — Pentagon explicitly denied. Iran state TV broadcast alleged captured US troops. Multiple IRGC claims later deleted after being disproven.

socialSources.claim: IRGC spokesperson claimed 650 US troops killed or wounded in first 2 days, including 160 at US military HQ in Bahrain. Claims largely disproven.
Claims Iran captured US soldiers. Pentagon explicitly denied any captures.
Sources in Bahrain report 12+ additional US wounded at 5th Fleet HQ not in official Pentagon count.

parties.injuredNote: 380 wounded across 7 countries per Pentagon (Day 39 late PM). +15 wounded in Iranian drone strike on Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait (IRGC Wave 98 of True Promise 4). Most returned to duty. Previous: 365 through Day 37. Includes 20 wounded at Prince Sultan Air Base from E-3 AWACS destruction wave (2 seriously). Wounds include burns, TBIs, shrapnel.

parties.name: Lebanon

confirmed.source: Lebanese Health Ministry (Day 38 AM, April 6). 1,449 killed and 4,053 injured since March 2. +7 killed in Kfar Hatta (incl. 4yo girl, Lebanese soldier), +4 killed in Jnah near Beirut hospital (+39 wounded). Israel forces closure of Masnaa border crossing. 51 Hezbollah attacks on Israel over weekend.

estimated.source: Lebanese Health Ministry (1,127 confirmed, Day 27) + Alma Center Hezbollah fighter estimates (~350 killed)
estimated.note: 1,134 confirmed by Health Ministry (Day 35). Alma Center estimates 390+ Hezbollah fighters killed including ~1/3 Radwan special forces and senior commanders. IDF 162nd Division killed 40 Hezbollah fighters in Day 34 advance. Hezbollah southern front commander Hajj Yusuf Ismail Hashem killed in Beirut naval strike (April 1). IDF reached new section of Litani River, ordering demolition of homes and bridges. ~1M displaced. Ground operations intensifying.

rumored.note: Hezbollah fighter casualties not separately broken out in Health Ministry figures — actual Hezbollah dead could be 300-400+. IDF ground invasion (launched March 16) expected to significantly increase casualties. Rescue teams unable to access multiple strike sites in southern Lebanon. Some villages entirely cut off.

socialSources.claim: 26 killed in last 24 hours across Beirut, Nabatieh, Baalbek, Bint Jbeil. 1 million displaced — 20% of Lebanon's population.
One million displaced nationwide. 130,000+ in 600+ collective shelters. Humanitarian access increasingly restricted.
IDF claims approximately 200 Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2. Hezbollah deliberately does not announce its dead.

parties.injuredNote: 4,053 injured per Lebanese Health Ministry (Day 38 AM). +39 wounded in Jnah neighbourhood strike 100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital (April 6). +40 wounded in Tyre/Habbush strikes. 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers wounded at El Adeisse.
parties.displacedNote: 1.5M displaced per Lebanese authorities as of March 24 — approximately 1 in 4 Lebanese. UN warned nearly a quarter of the population is uprooted.

parties.name: Gulf States (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, etc.)

confirmed.source: Al Jazeera tracker + regional government reports (Day 39 PM, April 7). UAE: 13 killed, 221 injured since Feb 28. Saudi: 2 killed + Jubail debris fire (no casualties). Kuwait: 7 killed. Bahrain: 2 killed. Oman: 3 killed. Qatar: 0 killed. King Fahd Causeway temporarily closed. + Bahrain Defence Force (April 8). 2 Bahraini civilians injured in Sitra from drone interception debris.

estimated.source: Compiled from regional government reports and HRW (March 26)
estimated.note: Kuwait: 6 killed, 32 injured. UAE: 13 killed total, 221 injured, 498+ BMs, 2,141 drones, 23 cruise missiles intercepted. Saudi Arabia: 2 killed, 20 injured + Jubail SABIC debris fire. Bahrain: 2 killed, dozens injured. Oman: 3 killed, 15 injured. Qatar: 0 killed, 16 injured. Iran targeting airports, oil hubs, petrochemical plants. King Fahd Causeway closed April 7.

rumored.note: HRW reports majority of Gulf civilian victims are migrant workers — deaths may be underreported due to foreign national status. Some Gulf nations restrict casualty reporting. Air defense interception rates (92-95%) prevented much worse toll. Iran launched 1,800+ missiles and drones at UAE alone.

socialSources.claim: At least 11 civilian deaths and 268 injuries across GCC states. 10 of those killed are foreign nationals. Migrant worker deaths likely underreported.
Fujairah oil hub struck 2 consecutive days. Saudi al-Kharj compound hit with casualties. Full picture unclear as some nations restrict reporting.

parties.injuredNote: 298+ injured across GCC states (Day 34 PM). UAE: 191 (per Gulf News). Kuwait: 52. Saudi Arabia: 20. Qatar: 16. Oman: 16. Bahrain: dozens. Cumulative UAE air defense: 457 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles, 2,038 drones intercepted since Feb 28.

confirmed.source: Iraqi health authorities (Day 36, April 4). At least 109 killed, mostly PMF members killed in US strikes. Includes al-Qaim PMF HQ and 34th Brigade HQ in Mosul struck. Islamic Resistance claimed 19 drone/missile attacks on US bases in single day (April 4).

estimated.source: Iraqi health authorities + The National + Kurdistan 24 reporting
estimated.note: Most casualties are PMF (Popular Mobilisation Forces) members killed in US strikes on militia targets. Day 25 Habbaniyah strike added 15 killed (including commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji) and 30 wounded. CWO Arnaud Frion (France) killed at Makhmour base counted under France.

confirmed.source: French Ministry of Armed Forces (March 13). CWO Arnaud Frion, age 42, 7th Battalion Chasseurs Alpins, 21-year veteran, killed by Iranian Shahed drone at Makhmour base near Erbil, Iraq. First European military death in the conflict. Married, father of one.

estimated.source: French government confirmed single fatality. Pro-Iranian group Ashab Al Kahf claimed responsibility.
estimated.note: CWO Arnaud Frion killed at French/Peshmerga base near Makhmour, Iraq (March 12-13). 7 French soldiers total wounded. No Peshmerga injured. Macron called attack 'unacceptable.' Iraq PM Sudani vowed to prevent recurrence. France has ~800 troops across Iraq/Syria in Operation Chammal.
